name: Robledo - Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop
name_original: Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz — circular desde Robledo
description: Jog the moderate 7.5-mile Robledo - Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop, featuring tunnels, varied terrain, and diverse landscapes.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Robledo offer a diverse landscape, characterized by greenways, agricultural plains, and areas that historically featured oak groves. The region provides varied terrain suitable for running, from relatively flat paths along old railway lines to routes exploring local towns. These running paths allow for exploration of the natural and historical features of the area. Best jogging routes around Robledo The most popular jogging route is Robledo - Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop, a 7.5 miles (12.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a scenic greenway, offering varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is Robledo Town Hall loop from Robledo, an easy 2.7 miles (4.3 km) path. This route provides a pleasant run through the town, often completed in about 26 minutes. Local runners also love the Robledo Station – Robledo Town Hall loop from Robledo, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through local streets and natural surroundings, often completed in about 52 minutes. Jogging around Robledo is defined by greenways, agricultural plains, and local town paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy town loops to moderate greenway runs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 5 runners have used komoot to explore Robledo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Robledo?
A: Komoot currently features &lt;strong&gt;3&lt;/strong&gt; dedicated running routes around Robledo. These include options ranging from easy town loops to moderate greenway runs, offering diverse experiences for runners.

Q: Are there easy running paths for beginners in Robledo?
A: Yes, Robledo offers an easy running path perfect for beginners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31867646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo Town Hall loop from Robledo&lt;/a&gt; is a pleasant 2.7-mile (4.3 km) route that can be completed in about 26 minutes, providing a gentle introduction to running in the area.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Robledo?
A: Absolutely. All the featured running routes in Robledo are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084104909/robledo-sierra-de-alcaraz-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo - Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 7.5-mile (12.0 km) circular trail offering varied landscapes.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on a run around Robledo?
A: Running around Robledo offers a mix of scenery. You'll experience greenways, agricultural plains, and areas that historically featured oak groves, true to the region's name. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084104909/robledo-sierra-de-alcaraz-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you through varied landscapes, including the notable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2971463" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viaduct on the Via Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any interesting places to see along the running trails?
A: Yes, several points of interest can be found near or along the running routes. You might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo Station&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4080509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt;, which features the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5919080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor and Towers of Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2971463" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viaduct on the Via Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable landmark on one of the greenway routes.

Q: Are the running routes in Robledo suitable for families?
A: Yes, some running routes in Robledo are suitable for families, especially those looking for an easier pace. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31867646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo Town Hall loop from Robledo&lt;/a&gt; is an easy, shorter option that provides a pleasant run through the town, making it a good choice for family outings.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Robledo?
A: While the guide doesn't specify dog-friendly rules for each individual route, greenways and rural paths in Spain are generally accessible for dogs on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage and regulations, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near towns, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: How long do the running routes typically take to complete?
A: The running routes in Robledo vary in length and duration. The shortest, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31867646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo Town Hall loop&lt;/a&gt;, takes about 26 minutes. The longest, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084104909/robledo-sierra-de-alcaraz-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo - Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt;, typically takes around 1 hour and 16 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there options for public transport to access running trails in Robledo?
A: The guide mentions the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo Station&lt;/a&gt; as a point of interest and a starting point for one of the loops (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24528447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo Station – Robledo Town Hall loop&lt;/a&gt;). This suggests that public transport, likely a train or bus service, might be available to reach the station, providing access to nearby running trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Robledo?
A: Given Robledo's location in Spain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant running conditions with milder temperatures. Summer can be quite warm, especially in the middle of the day, while winter might bring cooler weather. The greenways and oak groves can provide some shade during warmer months.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Robledo?
A: More than 5 runners have used komoot to explore Robledo's varied terrain. They often praise the diverse landscape, which includes greenways and agricultural plains, offering a refreshing change of scenery. The mix of easy town loops and moderate greenway runs also provides options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any less crowded running options near Robledo?
A: While Robledo's routes are generally not heavily trafficked, for a potentially quieter experience, consider running during off-peak hours, such as early mornings or late afternoons. The greenway routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084104909/robledo-sierra-de-alcaraz-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt;, offer expansive sections where you can enjoy nature with fewer people.
tips:
The old Robledo station has been recovered as rural accommodation. It is the best option to spend the night without deviating from the greenway.

Just one kilometer away is the town of Robledo where there are bars, grocery stores and a pharmacy.

If your objective is simply to make a stop before continuing on your way, you can find a rest area with shade, drinking water and information signs a few meters from the road.
The Vía Verde has 25 tunnels along its route, some large and others small. In long tunnels, you will need lighting.
A small town where there are some shops and basic services, such as bars, restaurants, a pharmacy and a small grocery store. In the surroundings you can find numerous trails and hiking trails

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Robledo - Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: This moderate jogging loop is approximately 12 km long and generally takes around 1 hour and 16 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this greenway loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter sections of compacted earth and gravel, as well as more curvilinear and winding paths. The route features trenches flanked by carved stone walls and embankments, and you'll also pass through former railway infrastructure, including tunnels and viaducts. Some sections may involve agricultural plains, while others are more rugged and mountainous.

faqs.question: Is the Robledo - Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as 'moderate' and requires good fitness for jogging. While greenways are generally accessible, some sections near Robledo can be more challenging. If you're a beginner, be prepared for varied surfaces and some elevation changes, and consider a slower pace.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this loop?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends near the Robledo Station, which has been converted into rural accommodation. This is a convenient spot to park and begin your journey.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a transition from agricultural plains to a wilder landscape. Expect to see groves of holm oaks, junipers, and riverside vegetation like poplars and ash trees. The area also boasts impressive valleys, vertical scarps, and pine forests. Keep an eye out for natural features such as Arquillo Lake and the Laguna de los Ojos de Villaverde Natural Reserve, known for their biodiversity.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates several interesting features. You'll pass by the historic Robledo Station, a Small Tunnel on the Vía Verde, and the Robledo Town Hall. The trail itself is built on a former railway line, so you'll encounter old railway infrastructure, including tunnels (some unlit, so a torch is recommended) and viaducts.

faqs.question: Is the Robledo - Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other users, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this loop?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse scenery throughout the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for jogging, with lush greenery in spring and vibrant colors in autumn. Summers can be hot, so an early morning or late afternoon start is advisable if visiting then. Winters are typically mild but can be cooler.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee to use this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and jog the Robledo - Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop. It is a public greenway.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop is part of the larger Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz. It also intersects with sections of the Sendero de los Serranos and the Camino Robledo-Vía Verde, offering opportunities to explore further if you wish to extend your outing.

faqs.question: Are there any specific considerations for jogging through the tunnels on the Vía Verde?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail incorporates several tunnels from the old railway line. Some of these tunnels are unlit, so it's highly recommended to carry a headlamp or a torch, even during the day, to ensure your safety and visibility.

waypoints.name: Small Tunnel on the Vía Verde
